title: hypericum mutilum subsp. boreale
intro: Hypericum mutilum subsp. boreale, also known as Northern St. John's Wort, is a subspecies of the St. John's Wort plant, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and oval-shaped leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Hypericum mutilum subsp. boreale include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of depression. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or oil, which can be used topically or taken internally, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it medicinally.
